item.title: Cait Conley wins NY-17 Democratic primary, CBS News projects
item.description: Democrat Cait Conley will challenge Republican Rep. Mike Lawler in the battle for New York's 17th Congressional District, which encompasses many of New York City's northern suburbs.

item.title: Trump-linked firm is lobbying for pardons and its first client already paid $500K
item.description: Mo Strategies, started by former Trump campaign and administration officials, recently expanded its practice into the lucrative world of pardon lobbying.

item.title: Trump's refusal to sign housing bill casts shadow over meeting with GOP senators
item.description: President Trump's decision to abruptly cancel the signing of a landmark bipartisan housing bill marks the latest misalignment between him and GOP lawmakers.

item.title: Trump cancels housing bill signing, reiterates demand for SAVE America Act
item.description: President Trump canceled a planned signing ceremony on Wednesday for a housing affordability bill that passed Congress by wide bipartisan margins.

item.title: U.N. nuclear agency chief says Iran inspections will happen, at some point
item.description: After conflicting remarks from Iran and the U.S., the U.N. nuclear agency chief says Iranian sites will be inspected, but the timing is "not essential."

item.title: Air marshals warn drone operators to avoid World Cup restricted zones
item.description: Despite repeated warnings, many drone operators are allegedly violating flight restrictions near World Cup sites, the TSA says. Meanwhile, more drones are being integrated into the U.S. national airspace and military operations. Drone expert Tombo Jones joins "CBS News 24/7 Mornings" to comment on drone technology.

item.title: Gen. Chris Donahue set to retire, in latest departure by top military official
item.description: Gen. Chris Donahue had earned the ire of Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, multiple sources told CBS News.

item.title: Israel refuses to completely withdraw from Lebanon despite pressure from Iran
item.description: Iran wants Israeli forces to leave Lebanon as Tehran negotiates a deal with the U.S. While Israel says it is now considering a U.S.-backed proposal to remove some of its military presence, leadership is refusing to completely withdraw from the region. BBC News' Charlotte Gallagher has more.

item.title: What is the $5,000 rule for HVAC systems?
item.description: This simple formula can help you decide whether it's smarter to repair or replace your HVAC system.

item.title: Bill Gates testified that he was aware of Jeffrey Epstein's prior sex conviction
item.description: The House Oversight Committee released transcripts of Bill Gates' testimony in the Jeffrey Epstein probe. CBS News' Aaron Navarro has more.

item.title: Rubio meets with Gulf allies in Kuwait to garner support for Iran deal
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is in Kuwait on Wednesday to meet with Gulf allies as the U.S. moves to get closer to a deal with Iran. CBS News' Aaron Navarro has more.

item.title: Trump reels from Iran woes as he prepares for America 250; lawmakers continue probing Epstein case
item.description: President Trump says he ordered the Justice Department to investigate alleged gas price gouging amid negotiations with Iran. Meanwhile, new details have emerged on Bill Gates' congressional testimony about his links to Jeffrey Epstein. CBS News' Nikole Killion and Caitlin Huey-Burns report.

item.title: Who were the top NBA Draft first round picks?
item.description: AJ Dybantsa, Darryn Peterson and Cameron Boozer were the standout picks during the first round of the 2026 NBA Draft. Sports Illustrated's Derrick Parker breaks down the draft selections.

item.title: FBI, NYPD carry out searches tied to bribery investigation of police officials
item.description: The searches stemmed from an ongoing probe into the conduct of former NYPD Chief of Department Jeffrey Maddrey, a source told CBS News.

item.title: Our new podcast will help you make smarter "Money Moves"
item.description: Get clear, actionable financial advice starting June 30 wherever you get your podcasts.

item.title: Key highlights from critical New York, South Carolina primaries
item.description: All three New York City candidates who were backed by Zohran Mamdani are projected to win their Democratic primaries. Meanwhile, South Carolina Attorney General Alan Wilson won the Republican gubernatorial runoff to face Democratic Rep. Jermaine Johnson in November. CBS News' Anthony Salvanto and Skyler Henry have more.

item.title: Europe steams amid record-breaking heat wave
item.description: European officials are warning of a threat to life, especially for vulnerable people, as a dangerous heat wave passes over Europe. CBS News reporter Leigh Kiniry has the latest.

item.title: How artificial intelligence is being used to make advancements in healthcare
item.description: Dr. Pierre Elias, a cardiologist and medical director of AI at NewYork-Presbyterian and Columbia, explains the impact of artificial intelligence advancements in the medical field, including EchoNext that received FDA approval through Pathway Labs, and how we can use AI without leaning on it too much.

item.title: Latino voters could be key to midterms. What's driving them?
item.description: Leading Latino political strategists are examining the races where Latinos could play an outsize role in the midterm elections, and believe their votes will be critical in a number of House races.

item.title: Conor McGregor says "fame has its pitfalls" as he speaks about highs and lows of career
item.description: UFC champion Conor McGregor speaks to "CBS Mornings" co-host Nate Burleson about the highs and lows of his career as he prepares to return five years after retiring.

item.title: Progressives sweep NYC Democratic primaries as Mamdani proves power
item.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani proved his influence endures after three progressives he endorsed were projected to win their Democratic primaries. CBS News' Anthony Salvanto has more.

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that will help improve your everyday lifestyle. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: Cristiano Ronaldo scores double goals for Portugal in World Cup win against Uzbekistan
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo became the first player to score goals at six World Cup tournaments when he helped Portugal beat Uzbekistan 2-0. Adam Hunt, Chris Wittyngham, and Ian Joy of CBS Sports Golazo Network comment.

item.title: How you can recreate elements of a '90s summer in 2026
item.description: Parents are feeling nostalgic for summers they grew up with and are jumping on the trend to give their kids a '90s summer. It's a push to swap screens for activities. Emily Oster, the founder and CEO of Parent Data, speaks with "CBS Mornings" about the trend and what parents can incorporate.

item.title: Tech stocks tumble again as investors retreat from AI
item.description: Investors are recoiling from tech stocks again over doubts that AI spending will pay off in the long run. Yahoo Finance's Brooke DiPalma explains the current state of the global stock market.

item.title: Ex-Oklahoma inmate who was nearly executed 3 times gets new trial
item.description: A new murder trial is scheduled for Richard Glossip, a former Oklahoma death row inmate who was released on bond last month after being on the brink of execution three times.

item.title: Camp Mystic files for bankruptcy almost a year after deadly Texas floods
item.description: The camp listed its debt as exceeding $10 million.

item.title: Telescope snaps most detailed photo yet of Milky Way's heart
item.description: Euclid is on a mission to chart one-third of the sky in the hopes of shedding light on the enduring mysteries of dark matter and dark energy.

item.title: Alibaba sues Pentagon over blacklist designation
item.description: Chinese tech giant Alibaba has filed a federal lawsuit against the Defense Department for designating it a military-linked firm.

item.title: House approves major housing affordability bill, sending measure to Trump
item.description: The legislation aims to increase housing supply and lower costs. It marks a rare bipartisan legislative accomplishment for lawmakers.
